feed.title: Marat Saidov
feed.subtitle: My personal blog about AI and software development.

entry.title: A Chat-Based Job-Matching Agent
entry.summary: I was the technical lead on this project — the final project for the Introduction to AI Agents course at Data Sanity. I designed the end-to-end architecture, set the overall direction, and filled in the gaps across the stack as the team built it out. The code is on GitHub. The core idea: free text carries more signal than checkboxes. Intent like “a senior backend role, remote, fintech, that ...

entry.title: PyTorch Conference Europe 2026
entry.summary: I flew to Paris to attend PyTorch Conference Europe as a listener. Two days focused on talks around open source and what’s happening across the industry. What surprised me: remarkably little hype around agents, but a lot of concrete, practical content. Key highlights below, in order. 1. HuggingFace announced Kernels Hub. Why this matters: there are many accelerators, and for some of them ce...

entry.title: OpenTalks.AI 2026: Production-Ready Adapters for On-Device Language Models

link.title: OpenTalks.AI 2026: Production-Ready Adapters for On-Device Language Models

entry.summary: I spoke at OpenTalks.AI in Belgrade, Serbia. My talk covered the end-to-end journey of building production-ready adapters for on-device language models, from tuning through evaluation to real-world shipping. The session walked through several core areas: Adapter tuning for on-device models. How LoRA adapters are used to specialize a small base model (Phi Silica) for distinct skills — eac...

entry.title: Budapest Data ML Forum 2025
entry.summary: On June 18, I gave an online talk at the Budapest Data+ML Forum. The event is a solid, mid-sized conference that draws practitioners from Central Europe; it is a good place to exchange technical expertise with other engineers. My session focused on Phi Silica, a small language model designed to run entirely on Copilot+ PC laptops, and on Rewrite, a publicly available paraphrasing tool built on ...

entry.title: How to Automate Prompt Engineering with OPRO

link.title: How to Automate Prompt Engineering with OPRO

entry.summary: In the past few years prompt engineering has emerged as a critical technique for optimizing the performance of large language models (LLMs). For example, CoT with a help of reasoning models (e.g., DeepSeek) in a manual setting has proven to be a default approach to prompt engineering. Since a manual approach can consume a significant amount of engineer’s time, this sets the stage for introduci...
